title: Miconia leathyrsiflora
taxonomy: Miconia leathyrsiflora is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae. This family is commonly known as the "melastome" or "memecyclid" family. The Melastomataceae family is part of the larger order Myrtales, which includes other plant families such as the Myrtaceae and the Lythraceae. The Myrtales order is within the clade rosids, which also includes other plant orders like the Fabales and the Malpighiales. The rosids clade is part of the larger group of flowering plants known as the angiosperms.

The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, respiratory issues, and skin conditions.
